records.m_pText: A Changed City
Whoooa, this place is$nincredible!
Look at how shiny the floors$nare! I canвЂ” [PCM2], what are$nyou looking at?
Oh, uhвЂ¦various things. I was$ndeep in thought, you see.
Then why did I just catch you$nlooking at my reflection!?
ItвЂ™s differentвЂ¦ All of this is$ncompletely different than the$nlast time I was here!
I recall it being a two-tiered$ncity built upon the riverвЂ™s$nsurface, butвЂ¦
Before, the lower tier was$nresidential, while the upper was$nreserved for Crystalist$ninstitutions.
What of that giant stage at the$ntop? Did that serve a religious$nfunction?
IвЂ™ve no idea. Nor could I even$nguess what sort of place all$nthat neon is advertising.
Just what in the world could$nhave happened here?
The Water Vestal and Me
It sounds like you and the$nvestal of water were really$nclose.
Olivia was my one and only$nfriend. She is very dear to me.
We are the same age, and were$nselected as vestalings around$nthe same time.
Then we succeeded our$npredecessors and became full$nvestals together as well.
We were always together,$nwalking the same path in step.
I do not know that I could have$nendured the vestal training$nwithout her.
She was always the talented$none. Smarter than I, but kinder$nas well. She shone so bright.
There was a time we traded$nletters nearly every day.
Despite the distance separating$nus, she always felt close by.
Once we became vestals, our$nresponsibilities kept us from$nwriting as often, butвЂ¦
I never imagined anything like$nthis.
DonвЂ™t worry. IвЂ™m sure weвЂ™ll find$nher. SheвЂ™s probably busy$nlooking for you right now, too!
Yes, of course. And thank you,$n[PCM1].
A Pious Life
Not a person in sightвЂ¦
Still, itвЂ™s a relief to see the$ntempleвЂ™s unharmed.
The Temple of Wind was torn$napart. I was worried weвЂ™d find$nthe same here.
IndeedвЂ¦
At the very least, it seems the$nacolytes escaped harm. Or so I$nhopeвЂ¦
вЂ¦вЂ¦
The smell of the air here is so$nnostalgic.
I hear all four temples share a$nsimilar design. Perhaps they all$nfeel this way.
The construction is plain, just$nstone and simple windows. No$nornamentation to speak of.
But when the sun filters in, the$nprayer hall was suffused with a$ncalm warmthвЂ¦
I used to love just watching the$nlight pour through the$nwindows.
I can imagine how peaceful that$nwould be.
IвЂ™m certain it seems a dreadfully$ndull and monotonous life to$noutsiders.
But to meвЂ¦life in the temple$nwas rich, and perfectly$ntranquil.
TranquilityвЂ™s great, in$nmoderation.
But didnвЂ™t you ever get the urge$nto know what was going on in$nthe outside world?
It is not as if we completely$nforsook the world around us.
The world within the temple was$nsimply enough for us. ThatвЂ™s$nallвЂ¦
Two Tired Boys
YaaaaaawnвЂ¦
You s-saaaaaaid itвЂ¦
Yeesh, whatвЂ™s got you two$nyawning first thing in the$nmorning?
Actually, donвЂ™t answer that,$n[PCM2]. I can guess. But$n[PCM1], why are you tired?
IsnвЂ™t it obvious?
Those darn neon signs were$nflickering all night long.
Then as soon as I finally get to$nsleep, Mr. Night Owl here comes$nstumbling back to the roomвЂ¦
And we already know youвЂ™re a$nneurotic sleeper.
CanвЂ™t we call it a вЂњlightвЂќ sleeper?
Look, hereвЂ™s some coffee.$nThese are dire times,$nso I made it black.
Thanks, thatвЂ™s great.
Ha ha, look at this one.$nHe fell asleep still$nholding the cup.
DonвЂ™t go drowning in your$ncoffee, [PCM2].
TrГЁs Fashionaaabluh
Hmmm, whatвЂ™s fashionaaabluh$nhereвЂ¦
FashionвЂ¦what?
Fashionaaabluh. You know,$nfashionable? ThatвЂ™s how the$nhip kids said it back home.
Ooh, have a look at this.$nTadaaah!
An heirloom floral linen stole!
ThatвЂ™s lovely! And so$nfashionable!
Not fashionable.$nFashionaaabluh!
F-fashionaaabluh, thenвЂ¦$n[PCF2], where did you find it?$nIвЂ™m envious.
ArenвЂ™t you!? It was a gift from my$nmother, years and years ago.
If you wrap it around you$nlikeвЂ¦this! It makes for the$nperfect accent.
Absolutely fashionaaabluh!
YouвЂ™re always fashionable,$nthough, [PCF2].
Not fashionable!$nFASHIONAAABLUH!
Ha haвЂ¦
Although youвЂ™re nearly as$nfashion-minded as she is,$n[PCM2].
YouвЂ™re always fussing over your$nhair, and your shoes are$nblinding.
What can I say? I live my life in$nfront of the mirror.
A proper pompadour is a joy and$na responsibility. Shoes and$nbelt must match.
But thatвЂ™s only the beginning.$nYou donвЂ™t know the half of it.
Clothing I really like, I never$nwear. DonвЂ™t want to get it dirty,$ndo I?
What use are clothes you canвЂ™t$nwear?
See, [PCM1]?$nYouвЂ™re the only country bumpkin$nin the group.
Give this stole a try. Stoles on$nguys are in this season.
But this season is warm.
WhatвЂ™s the point of wearing a$nscarf when itвЂ™s not cold out?
Scarf!? ItвЂ™s not aвЂ¦$nHere, just try it!
At times one must suffer to be$nfashionaaabluh.
This has to look ridiculousвЂ¦$nOh, well. IвЂ™ll think of it as a$nsweat towel.
[PCM1], you look very,$nerвЂ¦f-fashionaaabluhвЂ¦
YouвЂ™d look right at home$nweeding a garden!
Sexy Clothes
IвЂ™m amazed an old man like the$nsage still travels town to town$nby foot.
Evidence of the manвЂ™s tireless$ncommitment to sexy clothes, my$ndear.
I knew from the moment I saw$nhim he was no ordinary grandpa.
Sooo, I have no problem with$nthe fact that we wound up out$nhere, butвЂ¦
Do you two really plan to put$n[PCF1] in something sвЂ¦sexy?
I fear I am ignorant in such$nthings. What makes a garment$nsexy, precisely?
Er, goodness,$nhow to put thisвЂ¦
Hold that thought! [PCF1], my$ndear, why spoil the surprise,$nhmm?
TodayвЂ™s вЂњblack as pitch list,вЂќ$nentry number oneвЂ¦[PCM2]!
FloremвЂ™s Finest Food
[PCF1], what is FloremвЂ™s finest$ndelicacy, food-wise?
Let me guess. вЂњI would dearly$nlove to escort youвЂ¦вЂќ Right?
Without question. But IвЂ™m also$njust plain hungry. ArenвЂ™t you?
Now that you mention itвЂ¦
So, [PCF1].$nAny recommendations?
Hmm, let me thinkвЂ¦ Florem is$nknown for stream noodles. Oh,$nand water jellies for dessert.
Those sound lovely, but have$nyou anything that sticks to the$nribs a bit more?
A bowl of eel wafers, perhaps?$nHmm, or I believe queen perch is$nin season.
Eel wafersвЂ¦bowl? What in the$nworld?
It is a delicacy made by stacking$nthin slices of eel from FloremвЂ™s$nmarshlands. Quite famous.
And whatвЂ™s a queen perch?
A river fish, prized for their$naroma. They swim upstream at$nthis time of year, and are$nserved salted or fried.
I think some fried queen perch$nsounds good, then.
Perhaps IвЂ™ll try these eel$nwafers, then.
Ooh, me too! Eel wafers, fried$nperch, and water jellies!
Ha ha, what, one of everything?
I Want This, and That, andвЂ¦
[PCF2], did you take$n[PCM2] off on another of$nyour shopping sprees?
Tee hee. What can I say?$nI see these clothes and$njust canвЂ™t help myself.
ThatвЂ™s abundantly clear,$nlooking at all the bags in$n[PCM2]вЂ™s hands.
My poor, numb handsвЂ¦
Wooorth iiit! I mean, just look$nat this adorable one-piece!
But [PCF2], you already own$na dress thatвЂ™s practically$nidentical to that!
Whaaat? TheyвЂ™re totally$ndifferent!
See? The length is shorter on$nthis one. ItвЂ™s all the rage.
If you go buying new dresses$nevery time hemlines change,$nyouвЂ™ll be broke in no time.
Yes, and my purse is in no$ncondition to support her little$nhabitвЂ¦ Heh.
Needless to say, mine is no$nfuller. I havenвЂ™t got two coins$nto rub togetherвЂ¦ Heh.
DonвЂ™t tell meвЂ¦
Thank you sooo much, [PCM1]!
Aaah! ThatвЂ™s MY wallet!
Anxiety
HmmmвЂ¦
Is something wrong?
Do you remember the girl who$nwon at the festival?
Sure. The little one, right?
I have no interest in little girls.$nThereвЂ™s nothing sexy about$na twig thatвЂ™s yet to bud.$nI canвЂ™t imagine how she won.
I donвЂ™t think [PCF2] was$nasking for all thatвЂ¦
I could swear IвЂ™ve seen her$nsomewhere before. I just canвЂ™t$nplace whereвЂ¦
[PCF1]вЂ™s Anguish
вЂ¦вЂ¦
[PCF1] is beyond$ndownheartedвЂ¦
I wish I knew what to say to her.
ItвЂ™s my fault. I should have$nremembered who Victoria was$nsooner.
The two vestals shared a bond$ncloser than I can begin to$nimagine.
For now, weвЂ™d best just give her$nspace.
вЂ¦вЂ¦
The Pendant
Are you alright, [PCF1]?$nIf youвЂ™re forcing yourself,$nyou donвЂ™tвЂ¦
вЂ¦Are you so worried about me?$nDo I make you so worried?
Of course IвЂ™m worried about you.$nAfter what happened, how could$nI not beвЂ¦?
I am fine. I havenвЂ™t the$nluxury of stopping to mope.
вЂ¦вЂ¦
When we first met, you were$npraying to that pendant of$nyours, werenвЂ™t you?
ThisвЂ¦ The sage gave this to me$nwhen I was a child.
He said something I didnвЂ™t$nproperly understand, then$nhanded it to me.
What did he say?
вЂњHave the courage to think and$nact on your own.вЂќ
вЂњAnd the courage to disobey as$nwell.вЂќ
вЂњAnd should you ever face a$ndanger beyond all hope, this$npendant will be your salvation.вЂќ
вЂ¦What do you think he meant?
I had no idea, at the time. Even$nnow, I cannot say that I$nunderstand any better.
The sage is a mysterious man,$nbut I trust his words were true.
вЂ¦Now, thatвЂ™s enough talk. We$nought to be moving.
A Foul Stench
Hey, does anyone else smell$nsomething funny?
Yeah. What IS that? ItвЂ™s strongвЂ¦$nAugh, itвЂ™s like somethingвЂ™s$nrotting. My eyes are watering!
Perhaps weвЂ™re smelling the$nMiasma WoodsвЂ™ namesake?
I doubt it. The wind is blowing in$nthe opposite direction.
I feel like itвЂ™s coming from the$nnorth.
[PCM2], when was the last time$nyou took a bath?
Heh, if it were my natural musk,$nyou and [PCF1] would be$nlosing your minds with desire$nabout now.
вЂ¦вЂ¦
вЂ¦Nothing? No response at all?
